seoTitle: How to Copy and Paste Text in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to copy and paste text in Canva while preserving formatting, styles, and fonts across multiple design projects.
intro: Copying and pasting text between Canva designs saves time and keeps your styling consistent across projects. This tutorial shows how to transfer text properly, including tips for preserving formatting along the way.

steps.heading: Navigate the Canva interface
steps.body: Get comfortable with where text elements live so you can locate what you want to copy.

steps.heading: Copy text within Canva
steps.body: Select the text you want to duplicate and copy it using Canva's built-in tools.

steps.heading: Paste text effectively
steps.body: Place the copied text into a new design or location while keeping formatting intact.

steps.heading: Review use cases and examples
steps.body: See how this feature helps when reusing content across multiple related designs.

faq.question: Does copying text preserve fonts and formatting?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial covers tips for maintaining formatting during the copy-paste process.

faq.question: Can I copy text between different Canva projects?
faq.answer: Yes, text can be copied and pasted across multiple design projects to save time and maintain consistency.

faq.question: Is this useful for team collaboration?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial includes best practices for collaborating with team members using Canva's copy and paste tools.

faq.question: What common mistakes should I avoid?
faq.answer: Watch for formatting mismatches or unintended style changes when pasting text between designs.
